rulings.Case Title: DAVID BRYAN VS. U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE FOR J.P. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff David Bryan'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Demand For Production And Inspection And Imposing Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 8, 2012, line 1. Plaintiff David Bryan'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Demand For Production And Inspection And Imposing Monetary Sanction Grant motion compelling further responses to Interrogatories. Demands 3,4,5,7 & 13 (production limited to Does that concern plaintiff's loan) Grant Sanctions in the amount of $4500 to be paid within 21 days from notice of entry of this order and furnishing further responses. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM KENNEDY VS. CHASE HOME FINANCE, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 8, 2012, line 2. DEFENDANT CHASE HOME FINANCE, LLC DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend as to all 8 causes of actions. 1st Injunctive relief not cause of action and not likelyhood of prevailing. 2nd failed to amend as ordered by court. 3rd and 8th fails to plead any facts suggesting, content or quasi contract. 4th Bank did not initiate foreclosure & notice of defaul not filed. See Aceves v. U.S. Bank. 5th Failed to amend as ordered by the court. 6th 2925.5 not applicable no notice of default and no initiation. 7th Per Citibank v. United Omaha Life Insurance, plaintiff can not plead around deficiencies by claiming B & P 17200 Violation. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MONTEJANO ARACELY RODRIGUEZ VS. THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON FKA THE BANK OF NEW et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 8, 2012, line 6. Defendants' Demurrer To Complaint. Off calendar. First Amended Complaint filed. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 8, 2012, line 5. DEFENDANT DARRAN OLIVER DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend. Notice fatally defective. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JUDITH STOCKTON VS. MARIANGELA URQUIZO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Pltfs Further Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 8, 2012, line 6. DEFENDANT MARIANGELA URQUIZO's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Further Discovery Responses Sustained with leave to amend as to Causes of action 1 thru 5. These causes of action fail to set forth sufficient facts to support the alleged Causes of action. Sustained without leave to amend as to Cause of Action 6 because doesn't state any independent grounds of relief that is not duplicative of other causes of action. (501/REQ)
